quality_flags: risk-officer veto triggered (veto_flags non-empty) but writer action is 'sell' instead of 'avoid'

proposals.pattern: model_consensus bullish AND conviction_level in ['low', 'medium'] AND ma_state == 'bearish'
proposals.proposed_rule: Auto-set position_sizing_advice to '0%' and append 'quant_lag_trap' to veto_flags. Block any position > 0% unless conviction_level == 'high' or explicit catalyst confirmation exists.
proposals.rationale: Single case demonstrates quant strong_long with low conviction in a bearish MA regime is statistically noise. Codifying this CRO filter prevents quant alpha signals from overriding structural downtrends in future reports.

proposals.proposed_rule: Force writer action to 'avoid' and position_pct to 0.0. Reject any downgrade to 'sell' or 'hold' when veto is active.
proposals.rationale: Current report shows veto triggered but action set to 'sell', creating workflow ambiguity. CRO veto must be binary and absolute to maintain risk hierarchy and prevent writer discretion from diluting hard risk rules.
